strings.tutorial_body:
This tool predicts every possible reward before you spend a single gold.

[b]How to read it:[/b]
Each row shows what you'd get after clicking gold that many times first. Row 0 = click right now. Row 5 = click 5 golds first, then pick. Higher row = more gold spent first.

[b]How to use it:[/b]
[color=#FF6B35]1. Click any cell to mark a reward as your target[/color]
[color=#FF6B35]2. Or use the dropdown filters to auto-plan a path[/color]
[color=#FF6B35]3. Follow the green plan shown at the bottom[/color]

[b]Manual selection rules:[/b]
• Most columns allow 1 pick — clicking new replaces old
• Common Potion allows 2 picks — deselect first to replace

[b]Pro tips:[/b]
• Use the dropdown filters to instantly find and plan a path to any reward
• Hover any card/relic name for details; right-click for a full preview popup
• Drag panels to reposition; click ▼ to collapse

Pure info tool — does not change any game mechanics.
strings.error_gold_limit: Not enough fillers: need {0} offset gap, available stones exhausted
strings.error_conflict: Conflict: offset {0} is reserved by {1} plan
strings.error_no_path: No feasible path found (cannot reach all targets from offset {0}) — try ↻ reset
strings.error_potion_overflow: Not enough potions: need {0} reveals but only {1} items left
strings.error_potion_index_conflict: Potion conflict: both columns selected at same index ① — only one can be revealed first
strings.legend_costs: RNG: Card+6 Relic+1 Gold+1 Potion+1 Curse0 | Grid cost: Card 2×2=4 Relic 4×4=16 S.Gold 1×1=1 B.Gold 2×1=2 C.Potion 1×3=3 R.Potion 2×2=4
strings.legend_order: Same click: L→R→T→B→TL→BL→TR→BR→center; item triggers when last cell clears (hover for preview)
strings.legend_filter: Path: DP min grid cost. Prefers small golds (1 cell=1 offset). Potion selection auto-expands prerequisites (#3→#1→#2). Candidate offsets tried nearest-first.
strings.legend_substitute: Same-offset items are interchangeable: gold/potions/relics = +1, cards = +6. Swap freely during play.
strings.plan_long_warning: Many steps — verify path feasibility carefully!
